article_title: Qilin Ransomware Group Targets German Political Party Die Linke, Claiming 1.5 Terabytes of Stolen Data

content_preview.title: Qilin Ransomware Group Targets German Political Party Die Linke, Claiming 1.5 Terabytes of Stolen Data
content_preview.summary: Qilin ransomware group claims attack on German political party Die Linke, threatening to leak 1.5 terabytes of internal data in what the party calls a hybrid warfare operation.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
The Qilin ransomware group has claimed responsibility for a cyberattack on Die Linke, one of Germany's major political parties, adding the organization to its dark web leak site on April 1 after gaining access to internal systems days earlier. The incident marks a notable escalation in ransomware operations targeting political institutions in Europe.

## The Attack

editor_notes.content_quality: Solid cybersecurity article with attack timeline, party response, and threat actor context.
editor_notes.source_verification: BleepingComputer: verified — Qilin attack March 26, Die Linke confirmation March 27, 1.5 TB claim April 1, membership database safe, criminal complaint, hybrid warfare quote confirmed. CSO Online and Infosecurity provide additional context.
